reasons: Diggie reduces the value of Minsitthar's pick and teamfight control by protecting allies during the engage window.
Minsitthar depends on locking a target long enough for follow-up, while Diggie makes that timing harder.

proof.summary: Minsitthar wants to chain pull and zone control, while Diggie can remove debuffs from nearby allies and grant control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Minsitthar's real engage.
Diggie stays close enough to the target Minsitthar wants to catch.
failureCases: Minsitthar baits Diggie ultimate before committing.

reasons: Valir can push Minsitthar away before he lands a clean Spear of Glory or ultimate zone.
Valir's self-cleanse timing makes him harder to lock down if Minsitthar catches him.

proof.summary: Minsitthar wants to start a close-range lock zone, while Valir can knock enemies back and cleanse control to reset spacing.
worksBestWhen: Valir keeps distance and saves knockback for Minsitthar's commit.
Valir avoids standing where Spear of Glory can start the fight.
failureCases: Valir wastes knockback before Minsitthar engages.
Minsitthar catches Valir while cleanse is unavailable.

reasons: Pharsa can pressure Minsitthar from outside his preferred engage range.
Minsitthar becomes less reliable if he cannot force enemies into Spear of Glory or King’s Calling range.

proof.summary: Minsitthar wants enemies close enough for pull and anti-blink zone, while Pharsa can contribute damage from safer backline positions.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa keeps terrain and frontline between herself and Minsitthar.
Pharsa does not channel from predictable locations.
failureCases: Minsitthar reaches Pharsa before she can reposition.
Pharsa channels without vision against flank threats.

reasons: Lunox can punish Minsitthar after he commits if she avoids the first catch window.
Her defensive timing helps her survive predictable engage attempts.

proof.summary: Minsitthar is strongest during his pull and ultimate window, while Lunox can use defensive timing to avoid or punish the commit.
worksBestWhen: Lunox saves defensive timing until Minsitthar commits.
Lunox avoids fighting inside King’s Calling without support.
failureCases: Lunox is caught before activating defensive timing.
Minsitthar's team layers follow-up control after her timing ends.

reasons: Karrie can punish Minsitthar's fighter/frontline durability with true damage.
She works best when she kites outside King’s Calling instead of trying to dash through it.

proof.summary: Minsitthar wants to survive long enough to control a fight, while Karrie can pressure durable targets through true-damage Max HP stacks.
worksBestWhen: Karrie keeps enough spacing to avoid Spear of Glory.
failureCases: Minsitthar pulls Karrie before she can stack damage.
